introduction: Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) is a retrovirus, which infects primarily CD4 T cells (T helper cells) and leads to progressive destruction of the immune system with a consequent myriad of opportunistic infections and the development of certain malignancies. Acquired Immuno Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS) is defined as the presence of an AIDS-defining illness with a positive antibody test for HIV. HIV transmission primarily occurs through sexual transmission, blood transfusions, contaminated instruments, from mother to child during pregnancy, delivery, and breast-feeding.

features: Seroconversion - detectable by a positive HIV antibody test
Gradual decline in CD4 cell counts
Clinical Stage I - Asymptomatic, generalized lymphadenopathy, normal activity
Clinical Stage II - Weight loss < 10% of body weight, muco-cutaneous manifestations, Herpes zoster, respiratory infections, symptomatic normal activity
Clinical Stage III - Weight loss > 10%, unexplained chronic diarrhoea or fever, oral candidiasis, pulmonary tuberculosis, severe bacterial infections, bedridden < 50% of the day
Clinical Stage IV - HIV wasting syndrome, opportunistic infections, neoplasms, bedridden > 50% of the day

investigations: Renal function tests: Electrolytes, Urea and Creatinine
HIV DNA (paediatric diagnosis <18 months of age)
Genotype and phenotype assays for resistance testing
Pregnancy assessment, family planning and counselling services

adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Dolutegravir (DTG): CNS symptoms, GI symptoms, Hepatic symptoms, Hypersensitivity reactions
Nevirapine (NVP): Life-threatening skin rash, DRESS syndrome, Hepatitis
Efavirenz (EFV): Rash, CNS side effects, Contraindicated in pregnancy
Zidovudine (ZDV): Bone marrow suppression, GI intolerance
Stavudine (d4T): Peripheral neuropathy, Lactic acidosis, Peripheral fat atrophy
Didanosine (ddI): Pancreatitis, Peripheral neuropathy, Lactic acidosis
supportive_measures: Mechanical support and prophylaxis for opportunistic infections as dictated by the stage and CD4 count.

complications: PGL, Guillain-Barré syndrome, myopathy, aseptic meningitis
Pneumococcal/bacterial pneumonias, pulmonary TB, Herpes zoster
Miliary/extra-pulmonary TB, PCP, disseminated histoplasmosis and coccidiomycosis, PML
Wasting, peripheral neuropathy, HIV-associated dementia, cardiomyopathy
Disseminated cytomegalovirus, disseminated Mycobacterium avium complex
prevention: ART for pregnant and breastfeeding women
Behavioural interventions: Abstinence, fidelity, condom use, delay sexual activity
